access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (EPS contracts with IPS/distributors under PBS/UPC) + reimbursement (recobro/MIPRES for non-PBS) + judicial (tutela when EPS denies/delays); de-facto shortages driven by EPS-IPS-distributor debt cascade
procurement_channels: EPS-PBS (covered by UPC capitation per affiliate); MIPRES/non-PBS (paid via presupuestos máximos to EPS for high-cost oncology drugs not on PBS); private pharmacy (out-of-pocket); tutela (court-mandated delivery when EPS fails). EPS contracts directly with pharmaceutical distributors (gestores farmacéuticos / operadores logísticos) and IPS for outpatient and inpatient oncology dispensing.
key_actors: EPS (Entidades Promotoras de Salud) — purchasers/insurers; principal EPS in 2025: Nueva EPS, Sanitas, Compensar, SURA, Salud Total, Famisanar, Aliansalud, Capital Salud (~28 authorized in 2025). IPS (Instituciones Prestadoras de Servicios) — provider hospitals and oncology centers. Pharmaceutical distributors (gestores farmacéuticos, e.g., Audifarma, Cruz Verde, Cafam, Colsubsidio). ADRES (Administradora de los Recursos del Sistema General de Seguridad Social en Salud) — pays UPC and presupuestos máximos. Superintendencia Nacional de Salud — supervises EPS solvency. MINSALUD — sets PBS via Resolution 2718/2024.
formulary_coverage: Most conventional oncology drugs (cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin) are PBS-covered through Resolution 2718/2024 high-cost disease provisions. Trastuzumab is covered for approved oncology indications. Newer high-cost oncology drugs (immunotherapy, targeted therapies) typically routed through MIPRES (non-PBS) prescription, often triggering tutela when delayed.
judicial_access_volume: Health-related tutelas: ~265,000 (2024) increasing to ~312,500 (2025); ~34% of all tutelas filed in Colombia are health-related. Defensoría del Pueblo accompanied 18,451 tutelas Jan-Aug 2025, with ~24% related to denial of medication delivery. National Health Superintendency recorded ~685,000 medication-related complaints; Defensoría received 35,000 complaints in 2024 alone (+32% vs 2023).
registration_timeline_generic: Not applicable (handled by INVIMA, not EPS).
biosimilar_pathway: Not applicable to EPS directly; biosimilar uptake by EPS varies and is influenced by price differential and IETS recommendations.
reform_status: in_litigation / partially_suspended — Decree 0858/2025 sought to restructure EPS role into Gestoras de Salud y Vida; Council of State suspended Decree 0858 provisionally on October 23, 2025. Petro administration's broader health reform was blocked by Congress in December 2025. EPS continues operating under existing model.
currency_mechanism: Floating COP. EPS pay in COP; FX volatility passes through to imported oncology drug pricing.
pharma_import_cost_impact: [uncertain] — not specifically quantified for EPS oncology procurement; ~70% of pharmaceuticals imported to Colombia, exposing EPS to FX-driven cost growth.
financial_mechanism: UPC (Unidad de Pago por Capitación) capitation funds PBS oncology drugs; recobro via presupuestos máximos for non-PBS (MIPRES); tutela-ordered drugs cross-financed. EPS payment cycles to distributors are 60-180+ days, creating de-facto credit on suppliers.
payer_debt_crisis: EPS-IPS total debt rose to COP ~32.9 trillion in 2024 (vs COP 25 trillion in 2023). Pharmaceutical portfolio component is COP 4.2 trillion. ~80% of EPS lack legally required reserves; only 6 of 29 EPS comply with financial regulations as of 2025. Nueva EPS reported COP 9.2 trillion in commitments (April 2025); EPS Sanitas COP 2.88 trillion. Government paid 90.3% of pharmaceutical operator invoices in 2024 (COP 13.3 trillion of 14.7 trillion filed) yet supply failures persisted, indicating intermediary blockages.
shortage_rate: Not a single national figure. Defensoría del Pueblo reports 90% of patients in some surveys did not receive prescribed medications timely; INVIMA listed 8 active shortages, 5 at-risk, 181 monitored as of Dec 2024. PQRS related to non-delivery of medications hit historical peak of 52,655 in January 2025.
shortage_causes: Primary structural cause: EPS-IPS debt cascade > pharmaceutical distributors withhold supply > IPS cannot dispense. Secondary: concentration of pharmaceutical distribution market eliminating competition; INVIMA backlog delaying new entrants; political-electoral struggle over health reform creating supply uncertainty. Health Superintendency 2025 inspections found tons of medicines stored in private warehouses near expiry — confirming financial blockade rather than physical shortage.
shortage_monitoring_system: INVIMA Desabastecimiento module since 2018; new MINSALUD SISCAATS system in development (proposed Resolution late 2025). Defensoría del Pueblo also tracks PQRS via SuperSalud channel.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: Hundreds of cancer patients reportedly forced to suspend chemotherapy due to non-delivery from EPS/distributors during 2024-2025 (People's Health Movement, ColombiaOne reporting). Specific cisplatin/doxorubicin/carboplatin/trastuzumab line items not separately identified in public INVIMA shortage list during this window. [uncertain on individual oncology drug events]
source_citation: 1) MINSALUD, 'La deuda acumulada de las EPS asciende a $32 billones de pesos,' 2025, https://www.minsalud.gov.co/CC/Noticias/2025/Paginas/deuda-acumulada-de-EPS-asciende-a-32-billones-de-pesos.aspx. 2) People's Health Movement, 'Medicine shortages and the political and electoral struggle in Colombia,' 2025, https://phmovement.org/medicine-shortages-and-political-and-electoral-struggle-colombia. 3) ColombiaOne, 'Is Colombia's Healthcare System on the Verge of Collapse?,' July 2025. 4) Colombia Reports, '80% of Colombia's healthcare intermediaries lack legally required funds,' 2025. 5) Holland & Knight, 'Decision of the Council of State Leaves Other Key Measures of the Ministry of Health in Limbo,' Oct 2025.
source_type_granular: government_primary (MINSALUD, Defensoría) + investigative_news (ColombiaOne, Colombia Reports, El Tiempo) + NGO/advocacy (People's Health Movement)
data_reliability: high for debt and tutela figures (primary government sources); medium for oncology-specific shortage causation
